Title: Validate matching address values for a single record
Description: Verify that when the Silver and Gold layer hold the same address value for a record, the validation completes successfully without flagging a mismatch.
Steps: 1. Create or select a record with identical address values in Silver and Gold. 2. Run the validation for that record. 3. Review the validation output.
Expected Results: The validation returns a Pass status for the record, and no mismatch is reported.

Title: Detect mismatched address values between layers
Description: Confirm that the validation identifies records where the Silver layer address and Gold layer address differ.
Steps: 1. Create or select a record with different address values in Silver and Gold. 2. Run the validation. 3. Review the resulting mismatch list.
Expected Results: The record is flagged as a mismatch and included in the failed validation results.

Title: Include record identifier and compared addresses in validation output
Description: Verify that each validation result includes the record identifier and the address values that were compared.
Steps: 1. Run validation on a known set of records. 2. Inspect the output for one validated record. 3. Check the output fields.
Expected Results: Each result entry contains the record identifier, the Silver address, and the Gold address.

Title: Support full data set and incremental validation execution
Description: Validate that the process can run for the complete dataset and for incremental updates without redesign.
Steps: 1. Execute the validation for a full dataset. 2. Execute the validation for a small incremental update set. 3. Compare both outputs.
Expected Results: Both execution modes complete successfully and produce validation results that are consistent with the selected scope.

Title: Return a clear pass or fail status per record
Description: Ensure that each record receives a clear status indicating whether it passed or failed validation.
Steps: 1. Run validation against a mixed set of matching and mismatched records. 2. Review the status field for each record. 3. Compare the statuses to the known data.
Expected Results: Each record is returned with a clear Pass or Fail status.

Title: Apply CR-specific comparison rule
Description: Verify that the validation logic follows the agreed business rule for address comparison defined in the CR.
Steps: 1. Configure the validation rule set for this CR. 2. Run the validation on sample records. 3. Compare the results against the expected business interpretation.
Expected Results: The validation uses the CR-defined comparison rule consistently for all checked records.

Title: Ensure deterministic validation behavior across runs and environments
Description: Confirm that identical input data produces identical validation outcomes across repeated runs and environments.
Steps: 1. Run the validation twice in the same environment using identical data. 2. Run it in a second environment. 3. Compare the results.
Expected Results: The validation results are consistent across repeated runs and environments.

Title: Handle equivalent formatting differences correctly
Description: Verify that expected formatting differences are treated as equivalent according to the business rule.
Steps: 1. Use two records where the addresses differ only in formatting such as case or spacing. 2. Run the validation. 3. Review the result.
Expected Results: The system treats the formatting differences as equivalent and does not falsely mark the record as a mismatch.

Title: Report blank and null address values distinctly
Description: Ensure empty, null, or missing address values are handled as a distinct validation outcome and reported clearly.
Steps: 1. Create records with blank, null, and missing address values in one or both layers. 2. Run the validation. 3. Review the output.
Expected Results: The validation marks these cases distinctly and clearly identifies the issue as missing or blank data.

Title: Support future CR rule updates without redesign
Description: Verify that the validation logic can be updated if the business rules change without requiring a process redesign.
Steps: 1. Review the current rule configuration. 2. Simulate a rule change in the configuration. 3. Run the validation again.
Expected Results: The validation logic can be updated through configuration or rule changes without redesigning the entire process.

Title: Include source record identifier in mismatch reports
Description: Confirm that each mismatch report identifies the source record that failed validation.
Steps: 1. Generate a mismatch for a known record. 2. Open the mismatch report. 3. Check the record identifier field.
Expected Results: Each mismatch report includes the source record identifier.

Title: Include validation run timestamp and run identifier
Description: Verify that each mismatch report contains the timestamp and run identifier for the execution.
Steps: 1. Run the validation with a defined run context. 2. Generate the mismatch report. 3. Inspect the report header fields.
Expected Results: The report includes the validation run timestamp and run identifier.

Title: Categorize mismatch cause as missing value formatting issue or real difference
Description: Ensure the report clearly indicates whether the mismatch was caused by a missing value, formatting issue, or a true data difference.
Steps: 1. Create three sample mismatches representing missing value, formatting issue, and real difference cases. 2. Generate reports. 3. Review the cause field.
Expected Results: Each report clearly identifies the mismatch cause category.

Title: Provide human-readable and downstream-consumable report formats
Description: Verify that the mismatch report is available in a human-readable form and can be consumed by monitoring tools.
Steps: 1. Generate the report. 2. Review the human-readable view. 3. Export or consume the same report through a downstream interface.
Expected Results: The report is readable by users and also consumable by downstream monitoring tools.

Title: Highlight affected records for remediation
Description: Ensure that the report makes the affected records easy to identify for review and remediation.
Steps: 1. Generate a report with multiple mismatches. 2. Review the record highlighting or prioritization fields. 3. Check whether the affected records are easy to locate.
Expected Results: The report clearly highlights the affected records so review and remediation can be completed efficiently.

Title: Block or flag unresolved mismatches from publication
Description: Verify that records with unresolved mismatches are not published as valid data without review.
Steps: 1. Create a record with an unresolved mismatch. 2. Run the downstream publish workflow. 3. Observe whether the record is released.
Expected Results: The record is blocked or flagged for review and is not treated as valid for publication.

Title: Apply validation status such as Failed Needs Review or Quarantined
Description: Confirm that mismatched records are tagged with an appropriate validation status.
Steps: 1. Create a failed validation case. 2. Review the record status assigned by the workflow. 3. Verify the status value.
Expected Results: The system assigns a validation status such as Failed, Needs Review, or Quarantined.

Title: Allow downstream processes to identify and exclude failing records
Description: Validate that downstream publish or consumption processes can recognize and exclude records that failed validation.
Steps: 1. Run the validation and then trigger the downstream consumer workflow. 2. Observe which records are processed. 3. Check the filtering logic.
Expected Results: The downstream process identifies and excludes records that fail validation.

Title: Surface alert or status indicator for operational teams
Description: Ensure that a failed validation produces an alert or visible status indicator for the responsible teams.
Steps: 1. Create a failed validation case. 2. Trigger the operational alert path. 3. Review the visible indicator.
Expected Results: A visible alert or status indicator is produced for the relevant operational teams.

Title: Prevent incorrect address values from propagating into Gold layer
Description: Verify that validation failures prevent invalid address values from entering the Gold layer.
Steps: 1. Create a record with a mismatch and a downstream publish step. 2. Run the validation and publish workflow. 3. Inspect the Gold layer output.
Expected Results: The invalid address values are not propagated into the Gold layer when validation fails.

Title: Create an audit entry for each validation run
Description: Ensure that every validation run creates an audit record with a unique run identifier.
Steps: 1. Execute the validation process. 2. Open the audit log. 3. Check for a new entry.
Expected Results: Each validation run creates an audit entry with a unique run identifier.

Title: Record run metadata in the audit entry
Description: Confirm that audit entries include run timestamp, source table, target table, record count, mismatch count, and status.
Steps: 1. Run a validation against a known dataset. 2. Inspect the generated audit record. 3. Verify each required metadata field.
Expected Results: The audit entry contains the run timestamp, source table, target table, record count, mismatch count, and status.

Title: Support later review of validation trends
Description: Verify that stored validation results can be reviewed later to understand recurring issues or trends.
Steps: 1. Run the validation multiple times over time. 2. Open the historical results. 3. Compare outcomes across runs.
Expected Results: The audit and results history can be reviewed later to identify trends or recurring issues.

Title: Provide traceability back to the change request and affected records
Description: Ensure that audit data can be traced back to the specific CR and the affected records.
Steps: 1. Run validation for the CSR-522 flow. 2. Open the audit data. 3. Check the linkage fields.
Expected Results: The audit log supports traceability to the change request and the affected records.

Title: Link failed or corrected records to remediation actions
Description: Verify that failed or corrected records can be associated with remediation actions for future reference.
Steps: 1. Create a failed record and a corrective action. 2. Link the remediation action to the record. 3. Review the audit trail.
Expected Results: Failed or corrected records can be linked to future remediation actions in the audit trail.
